reasoning: The PKGBUILD executes network requests (curl) in global scope during sourcing, creating supply-chain risks. This violates Arch packaging best practices by introducing dynamic behavior before build steps.
disagreement_analysis: Deepseek failed to complete analysis due to symlink loop error, while Qwen properly identified the critical PKGBUILD risk
coverage_issues: deepseek failed to review any files due to symlink error
Neither model reviewed .install scripts or source code (though PKGBUILD was primary risk)
false_negatives: deepseek missed PKGBUILD risk due to analysis error (symlink loop)
learnings: Improve error handling for symlink loops during source inspection
Prioritize PKGBUILD global scope commands as high-risk targets
Train models to recognize dynamic URL resolution in PKGBUILD global scope as unsafe pattern
Ensure mandatory PKGBUILD review even if other file analysis fails
